slides.title: Catching Specific Exceptions
Multiple Catch Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
class MathException extends Exception {}
class DivideByZeroException extends MathException {}
function divide($a, $b) {
    if ($b == 0) {
        throw new DivideByZeroException('Cannot divide by zero');
    }
    return $a / $b;
}
try {
    echo divide(10, 0);
} catch (DivideByZeroException $e) {
    echo "Math error: " . $e->getMessage();
} catch (MathException $e) {
    echo "Other math error: " . $e->getMessage();
} catch (Exception $e) {
    echo "General error: " . $e->getMessage();
}
?>

quizData.question: What happens if an exception is thrown that doesn't match any catch block?

options.text: It's caught by a default handler

quizData.explanation: An uncaught exception results in a fatal error.
